title: Compose Number from Tens and Ones
description: Combine quantities of tens and ones to form a standard numeral.
condition: Given a representation of a number in tens and ones (drawn or counted).
response: Add the tens and ones to write the total number.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Includes cases with 1 hundred 1 one = 101 and 1 hundred 15 = 115.

title: Count Forward by Ones
description: Generate the next number in a sequence by adding one.
condition: Given a counting sequence increasing by 1 with missing numbers.
response: Write the number that is one more than the previous number.
label: Production / Skill
example: In the sequence $99, 100, \_ , 102$, the missing number is $101$.
notes: Early counting skill; relationship may be verbalizable but often procedural.

title: Identify Triangular Prism by Properties
description: Select the solid that does not roll and has exactly two triangular faces.
condition: Given multiple 3D shapes and the properties 'does not roll' and 'has 2 triangular faces.'
label: Concept
example: Among a sphere, cube, and triangular prism, the triangular prism has 2 triangular faces and does not roll.

title: Read Numerical Value from Image
description: Extract a labeled number from a pictured object.
condition: Given an image of an object with a number label.
response: State the number shown on the label.
label: Production / Skill
example: The toy puppy image shows the number 18, so its cost is 18 points.
